ground_truth.title: Best budget headphones - Headphone Discussion / Help - HifiGuides Forums
ground_truth.author: Noah_B
ground_truth.main_content: A forum discussion thread seeking budget headphone recommendations under $100. The original poster (Noah_B) requests over-ear headphones with decent sound quality, passive noise cancellation, Bluetooth and wired connectivity, and no need for external amps. Community members recommend various options including Sony WH-CH720N, JBL Tune 710BT, AKG 361/371, and suggest considering IEMs (In-Ear Monitors) for better value. Another user (wlf_star) joins seeking similar budget closed-back headphones for home use, preferring something that can plug directly into a computer. The discussion covers trade-offs between on-ear vs over-ear, Bluetooth capability vs sound quality, and suggests looking at used or sale prices for better options within the limited budget.
with: I am looking for your recommendations for a new pair of headphones.
If 100$ is your budget i'd go for an IEM, you get a much better bang-for-the-buck.
I prefer the feel and sound of over ear headphones, so i'd rather get one of those.
No amp, I'd like something I can just plug into my computer with no fuss.
Used is probably the best bang for the buck you can get though i still would say look into iems.
